title: Analysis
description:
The primary interface between an external LLM and the wiki.

Produced by the external LLM after reading a source document and (optionally) calling `wiki context` to check for existing pages. Consumed by `wiki ingest`. See `docs/design/design.md` for the full contract.

source.description: Path or URL to the original source document.

claims.description: Factual claims extracted from the source.

concepts.description: Key concepts mentioned in the source.

data_gaps.description: Gaps in the source's coverage or evaluation.

suggested_pages.description: Pages to create, update, or append to.

contradictions.description: Contradictions detected against existing wiki pages. Should be empty if the LLM did not call `wiki context` first.

DocType.description: Document type — the external LLM classifies the source.

Claim.description: A single factual claim extracted from the source document.

confidence.description: How confident the external LLM is in this claim.

section.description: Section of the source document where the claim appears.

SuggestedPage.description:
A wiki page to create, update, or append to.

The wiki writes the page as `{slug}.md` with auto-generated frontmatter. `body` is plain Markdown without frontmatter — the wiki generates frontmatter from the other fields so the external LLM never needs to write YAML.

slug.description: Canonical file path relative to the wiki root (e.g. `concepts/mixture-of-experts`).

action.description: Whether to create, replace, or extend the page.

tldr.description: One-sentence summary surfaced in search results and frontmatter.

read_when.description: Conditions under which an LLM should retrieve this page.

Action.description: Action to take when integrating a [`SuggestedPage`] into the wiki.

oneOf.description: Create a new page. Fails if the slug already exists.
Replace the body of an existing page, merging frontmatter fields.
Add a new section to the end of an existing page's body.

Contradiction.description:
A contradiction between two claims from different sources.

Contradictions are first-class knowledge nodes — they are never deleted, only enriched. A resolved contradiction still carries the analysis that explains *why* the sources disagreed; that explanation is the knowledge.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the contradiction page.

source_a.description: Slug of the source page for claim A.

source_b.description: Slug of the source page for claim B.

dimension.description: The dimension along which the claims contradict.

epistemic_value.description: What the tension reveals that neither source captures alone.

resolution.description: Explanation of the resolution. Present only when `status` is `Resolved`.

Dimension.description: The dimension along which two claims contradict each other.

oneOf.description: Each claim holds in a different context or domain.
One claim was superseded by the other over time.
Each claim applies at a different scale.
Different measurement methodologies yield different results.
The field has not resolved this dispute.

Status.description: Lifecycle status of a contradiction node.

oneOf.description: The contradiction has been analysed and its resolution documented.
The contradiction is unresolved and awaiting enrichment.
An external LLM is currently enriching this contradiction.
